title: Reading and Parsing Customized XML

Reading and Parsing Customized XML

Reading and Parsing Customized XML

You can parse XML documents stored in files, fields, or variables using the UXMLREADER component API.

Before parsing a document, create a handler component that implements the SAX operations that are called when specific parser events occur, such as starting to read the document, encountering start and end tags, and error handling.

To read and parse an XML document:

  1. Create an instance of the UXMLREADER component.
  2. Create an instance of the handler component, or retrieve the instance name of an already active handler component.
  3. Activate the handler by calling the SETHANDLER operation.
  4. Start parsing XML documents using PARSE to parse a field or variable of type string or xmlstream, or PARSEFILE to start parsing a file.

    As the XML document is parsed, the operations in the handler instance are activated based on what the parser encounters in the XML document. When PARSE or PARSEFILE returns, either the entire XML document has been successfully parsed, or a fatal error has occurred, in which case a negative return value is returned.

    A fatal error can be caused by:

    • An XML document that contains invalid XML.
    • The SAX handler component is not available.
    • The UXMLREADER component instance is not available.
    • A handler component's callback operation returning a negative value or setting $status to a negative value.
Example: Current Component as Handler

The following ProcScript parses an XML file, using the current component as handler component. Because the current component is the SAX handler, it must have at least one of the supported SAX callback operations implemented.

operation ParseFile
 params
 string pInput : IN
 endparams
 variables
 string vReader
 endvariables
 newinstance "UXMLREADER", vReader
 activate vReader.setHandler($instancename) ; current component is Sax handler
 activate vReader.parsefile(pInput, "T")
 deleteinstance vReader
end

Example: Separate Component as Handler

The UXMLREADER cannot be used in a SOAP web service because the instance will not be available in the web service component. Therefore, the web service should call a Uniface service component on which the UXMLREADER is instantiated.

Alternatively, you can use a different component instance as handler, as shown in the following example, which parses a string parameter:

operation ParseInput2
 params
 string pInput : IN
 endParams
 variables
 string vReader, vInst
 endvariables
 newinstance "UXMLREADER", vReader
 newinstance $componentname, vInst
 activate vReader.setHandler(vInst)
 activate vReader.parse(pInput, "T")
 deleteinstance vInst
 deleteinstance vReader
end
Related Links
  • Creating a SAX Event Handler

    Before starting to parse XML documents using the UXMLREADER component, you need to implement the operations that handle parser events, such as starting and ending the document and elements, and error handling.
